test.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper" && cargo test && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/shared" && npx vitest run && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx vitest run && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx vitest run

typecheck.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/shared" && npx tsc --noEmit && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx tsc --noEmit && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx tsc --noEmit

lint.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx eslint src/ && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx eslint src/ && tmpdir=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmpdir" && apt-get download rust-clippy >/dev/null && dpkg-deb -x rust-clippy_*.deb "$tmpdir/extract" && PATH="$tmpdir/extract/usr/bin:$PATH" cargo clippy --manifest-path "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper/Cargo.toml" --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

blockingIssues.description: Attached-session forwarding polls the bounded `/sessions/:id/history` snapshot instead of consuming a lossless per-message stream, so bursty agent output can be evicted from the relay ring buffer before the bot fetches it.

Cancelled approvals are only cleared when the background forwarding poll later observes `turn/completed`, so a prompt sent immediately after approval cancellation can still be misclassified as a malformed `y`/`n` reply.
After the child exits, the wrapper aborts the relay task before draining buffered stdout, so a final `turn/completed` or `serverRequest/*` frame can still be dropped during shutdown.

Lightweight turn/input notifications are broadcast to every cached `lastSeenChats` entry except the actively attached user, leaking one user's session activity to unrelated users and violating multi-user isolation.

The bot integration suite imports `server/dist/relay-server.js` and launches `wrapper/target/debug/codex-relay-wrapper`, so it is not reproducible from a clean checkout where those ignored build artifacts have not been prebuilt.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Keep feature-to-assertion mappings and claimed coverage aligned with the behavior actually implemented and tested. Do not map a partial feature to an entire contract item or full assertion range unless the supporting behavior/tests are already present.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The bot-command-parser review found `VAL-BOT-001` was assigned before partial/ambiguous `/attach` behavior existed, and the integration-core-flows review found the feature metadata claimed `VAL-CROSS-001` through `VAL-CROSS-023` while only three scenarios were implemented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the current bot-facing relay surfaces and their delivery guarantees: `GET /sessions` returns summaries, `GET /sessions/:id` returns detail objects, `/events` carries lifecycle notifications, and `/sessions/:id/history` is a bounded ring buffer snapshot rather than a lossless attached-message stream.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-relay-list-schema review passed only after correcting the undocumented list/detail schema split, while the bot-attach-detach review found attached forwarding built on `/history` polling can silently lose bursty agent output because the relay history buffer is bounded.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the notification-scoping rule for multi-user bot work: lightweight session notifications must stay scoped to the intended recipient(s) and must never broadcast across unrelated users just because the bot remembers their last chat.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The bot-auto-detach-and-notifications review found the feature description's 'any registered session' wording was interpreted broadly enough to leak one user's session activity to every cached chat, conflicting with the existing multi-user isolation contract.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Either make the bot integration suite source-driven or encode its build prerequisites explicitly in the shared commands/init flow, because the current suite depends on prebuilt `server/dist` and wrapper debug artifacts that a clean checkout does not have.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The integration-core-flows review found `bot/src/integration-core-flows.test.ts` imports `../../server/dist/relay-server.js` and launches `wrapper/target/debug/codex-relay-wrapper`, but `test-bot` and `.factory/init.sh` do not build those artifacts first.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add the wrapper relay-shutdown invariant to the skill guidance: when wrapper features depend on forwarding stdout-derived lifecycle frames, workers must preserve relay delivery until stdout has drained, not abort the relay task immediately after `child.wait()` returns.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-wrapper-always-forward-lifecycle review found the detached-forwarding policy itself was correct, but the wrapper still aborts the relay path before draining final stdout output, which can drop the last lifecycle event on child exit.

rejectedObservations.observation: bot-command-parser's `/sessions` schema mismatch should remain an actionable scrutiny failure for the milestone.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as already remediated in the same milestone by the passing fix-relay-list-schema feature, so it is tracked as a superseded historical failure rather than a new fix request.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a shared bot service entry immediately to `.factory/services.yaml`.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected for now because the bot has no stable healthcheck/port contract and depends on external Feishu credentials; adding a nominal start command during scrutiny would be low-value and potentially misleading.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update architecture docs to declare `lastSeenChats` and `pendingApprovals` as the intended canonical bot runtime state.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the current `lastSeenChats` usage underlies the blocking notification-leak bug, so documenting that state as canonical now would be premature.

rejectedObservations.observation: Separate notes about REST schema duplication, missing attached-message delivery semantics, and notification scoping gaps should all be tracked independently.
rejectedObservations.reason: Consolidated into the broader AGENTS.md guidance updates above to avoid duplicating closely related relay/bot contract issues.
